key_note: Both rules carry -k access but are DIFFERENT rules (execve vs init_module/finit_module) with wildly different volumes (HIGH vs LOW). Per-rule classification must NOT be collapsed by the shared key: each rule keeps its own tier. In --from-log per-key aggregation, key=access sums BOTH rules' events into one bucket (correct for per-key), but the ruleset-only per-RULE tiering must still distinguish them.

rules.note: unrestricted execve HIGH. Shares key=access with the module rule but is a DISTINCT rule.
module-load syscalls, rare => LOW. SAME key=access. Must NOT be conflated with the execve rule despite the shared key.

rate_band_basis: f3 section 6: execve HIGH; module-load rare LOW. Two distinct rules, one shared key.
